title: Attestplane settlement_precondition_claim (v1)
description: Claim schema asserting that a chain segment satisfies the 'verify-before-settle' precondition: a lease_lifecycle_event {lifecycle: consumed} OBSERVATION precedes a settlement_event {lifecycle: requested} OBSERVATION for the same lease_id_hash, within the same chain. ADR-0009 § B.3 concept-only absorption — Attestplane substrate emits no settlements. The claim describes a CLAIM about the chain; verifier returns yes/no/reason_code based on observed events. Substrate NEVER executes settlement (REDLINE C.3 budget allocator + C.8 gateway command execution).

claim_schema_version.description: Independent of chain.schema_version / anchor_schema_version / signature_schema_version / reason_code_schema_version / lease_event_schema_version / policy_event_schema_version / replay_event_schema_version. Bump requires ADR amendment.

claim_kind.description: Future ADR may add sibling claim_kinds (e.g., approval_precondition). v1 ships only this one.

lease_id_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the lease whose 'consumed' event MUST precede the settlement_event in the chain. Same format/semantics as lease_lifecycle_event.lease_id_hash.

settlement_run_id.description: Opaque identifier of the settlement run. Verifier looks for a settlement_event in the chain whose payload has matching settlement_run_id. Substrate does NOT issue this identifier.

expected_settlement_amount_hash.description: Optional SHA-256 hex of the canonical-text representation of the expected settlement amount. Hash-only — actual amount body NEVER stored. Verifier may cross-check against an off-chain amount registry if available.

claim_observed_at.description: Caller-asserted RFC-3339 UTC timestamp when this claim was constructed. Substrate records as-is; for authoritative time use AnchorRecord (ADR-0003).
